programme.desc: A baseball player stands accused of killing his wife. However, when the jury members are taken on a tour of the crime scene, the defendant is murdered with a meat cleaver. Horatio sets about solving both deaths, but discovers the sportsman's defence team may have tampered with the evidence. Suspicion soon turns to the victim's daughter, while a notepad puts a juror in the frame.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The body of a woman is linked to a serial killer who induces comas in his victims and imprisons them. Taylor and Flack's team must piece together the evidence with the help of a survivor who can communicate only by blinking. With Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo, Vanessa Ferlito and Eddie Cahill.(n)

programme.desc: A military officer has a painful reunion with his daughter. Guest starring Daniel J Travanti (Hill Street Blues) and Lee J Cobb (The Exorcist) with real-life daughter Julie.(n)

programme.title: Gunsmoke

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: A Small Piece of the Pie
programme.desc: Neil Manson returns to Sun Hill to work on the murder of a gay man and Phil Hunter spots a pattern in similar recent crimes. Mark leaves his partner distraught when he moves out to find some space, and Cindy urges her reluctant husband to open the results of the paternity test.(n)

programme.desc: Abra tries to collect more HIV drugs from the pharmacist and performs an unnecessary operation on a patient, while Elliot reprimands Joseph again after catching him having a conversation with Martha. programme.title: Casualty

Meanwhile, a French exchange student collapses, and a widow takes a nasty fall. Medical drama, starring Derek Thompson and Cathy Shipton, with a guest appearance by Margi Clarke.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: When Justice Isn't Enough - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of three. An anonymous caller alerts Phil Hunter to common threads in recent murders, and the team starts looking for a serial killer on the Sun Hill gay scene. Lance Powell struggles to come to terms with Mark's decision to take time out from their relationship and decides to drown his sorrows, but a visit to the gay bar he recently investigated puts him in grave danger.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol's attempts to give Sonia a birthday she will remember go awry, Sanjay makes an enemy of Ian, and Pat is forced to face some home truths about life after Frank.(n)

programme.desc: Pauline's worried about Michelle and asks Mark to talk to her. Nigel finds out there's a thief about. Carol has problems with a criminal nearer home, and Phil fights with Jimmy.(n)

Anna and Kim welcome the latest recruit, excitable trainee Zoe Phelps, while the hospital staff attempt to make a young heart transplant patient's wish come true. Lesley Dunlop and Denise Van Outen star.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon makes a go of it at the cafe, but Tracey finds it hard to feel pleased for her. Meanwhile, Dorien becomes preoccupied with more romantic pursuits. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey finds herself under suspicion after a valuable piece of DeVere's antique china mysteriously disappears while she helps him catalogue his collection. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy makes an ill-advised attempt to establish long-distance communication with Compo and Clegg by investing in some walkie-talkies. However, it doesn't take long for one of the units to fall foul of a tractor. Long-running rural comedy, with Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo, Clegg and Foggy are intrigued by some perfectly respectable men who turn up without their trousers, before they take part in an event sponsored by a village charity.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A middle-class couple's adopted child is kidnapped, a case that takes Gently and Bacchus to a home where unmarried mothers are forced to give up their babies. Suspicion initially falls upon the natural mother, who is thought to have 'stolen' her baby back. But further investigations reveal a much darker side to the adoption home itself and raise questions as to how far this seemingly perfect couple were prepared to go to become parents. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Mark Gatiss, Helen Baxendale and Alison Steadman.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Ted leads an investigation into the apparent suicide of a city trader who was embroiled in the cut-throat world of London's financial district in the 1980s. As the team delve deeper into the macho fraternity of 'The Wolfpack', they find a darker side to the debauchery, secret hand signals and all-nighters that bonded the traders together - including a strict hierarchy that might be worth killing for. Meanwhile, Steve tries to learn some tricks of the trade, amid his own financial crisis. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team pursues a serial killer with a grisly desire to look at his victims' faces as they die, and the trail leads into the world of academic philosophy. programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.title: The Home That Two Built
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc narrates this look at how BBC Two programmes kept track of lifestyle trends in the 1970s, an era of dodgy fashion and stodgy food that had people escaping into a world of stripped pine, country kitchens and Laura Ashley. This episode also shows that the decade had many similarities to today, from house price inflation and soaring energy costs through to worries about the threat to 'real' food and environmental sustainability. Featuring contributions by Eve Pollard, Gyles Brandreth, Wayne Hemingway, Loyd Grossman and Alan Titchmarsh.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their tour around Britain, following Samuel Chamberlain's 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. This leg takes them to Bristol, where they rub shoulders with Elizabethan pirates and learn about an ancient system of cultivation that uses fish waste as fertiliser. They also find time to cook up some gujarati street food, take a trip back to the 1970s and help decorate a chocolate version of the SS Great Britain.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the steel city of Sheffield where he discovers an early 20th century steam engine which produced armour plates for a Royal Navy warship. Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines Jupiter, the largest and oldest planet in the solar system, revealing how its immense gravity influenced where other planets developed, destroying several nascent planets and negatively influencing the development of Mars. Today, Jupiter's great mass continues to be felt. Its gravity torments its moon Io, creating the most volcanically active body in the solar system. And through the vice-like grip it exerts over the asteroid belt, Jupiter's influence extends as far as Earth.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Britain Fed
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Ade Adepitan gain access to some of Britain's biggest supermarkets and their suppliers to see how they are dealing with the coronavirus outbreak, the most testing time in their history. Sara finds out what goes on in one supermarket over 24 hours, from opening to closing and all through the night, to see how they are coping during the crisis. Elsewhere, Ade finds out how the nations' farms, factories and depots have risen to the challenge to produce and supply our supermarkets with food in these exceptional circumstances.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: Josh's father is still in danger, and Lise must try to get there in time to save him. As the killer's motives become ever clearer, Cardinal and Delorme travel to Toronto to search his abandoned flat Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author Giles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: Cardinal and Delorme try to extract enough information from a suspect to get a lead on the killer, as he makes off into the depths of the forest with his last two chosen victims. Will they be able to stop him in time? Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author GIles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Irwins experience life in the 1970s, 1980s and 1990s, discovering how a new generation of black Britons made their way through three decades of British history, guided by presenter Giles Coren and social historian Emma Dabiri. They meet former West Indies cricket captain Clive Lloyd and learn about the 1976 tour of England in which he led his side to victory, before reflecting on the Brixton Riots of the 1980s, and hosting a typical 1989-style party with DJ and producer Jazzie B of Soul II Soul.(n)

programme.title: Soon Gone: A Windrush Chronicle
programme.desc: As the only white person in her husband's family, Samantha Williams has had to counter the racial assumptions from his folks and her own. Monologue, starring Montserrat Lombard.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Saying goodbye to Puducherry, the celebs journey north to the foothills of the Himalayas for their final week in India. Following a treacherous drive up narrow, winding roads, they arrive at their new home on the outskirts of Rishikesh, ready to experience the spiritual side of life in Northern India. After starting the next day with an outdoor group meditation session among the wild monkeys and spectacular views, Duncan and John waste no time exploring the bustling town of Rishikesh, which attracts thousands of pilgrims from all over the world.(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the south-western coastline of Scotland, with Mehreen taking to the skies with former RAF fighter pilot Archie Liggat, who is now using his skills to document large-scale pollution hidden along the coastline. Adrian, meanwhiile, visits Portpatrick to meet Calum Currie, who has persuaded his community to buy shares in the town's harbour - revitalising it for all who live there. Plus, Patrick Aryee joins volunteers help to track rare birdlife on the Ailsa Craig and Joe Lindsay visits an artists' colony that was the setting for cult film The Wicker Man.(n)

programme.title: The Home That Two Built
programme.desc: The documentary tracking 50 years of lifestyle and homemaking programmes on BBC Two heads into the Eighties, a decade when more homeowners meant more money was spent on houses than ever before. Ken Hom caused a culinary revolution through his time-saving wok, Jilly Goolden got the nation drinking wine, Keith Floyd took the drudgery out of cooking by making it entertaining and no-nonsense dog-trainer Barbara Woodhouse became an unlikely star. Mel Giedroyc narrates, with contributions by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Eamonn Holmes, Esther Rantzen and Alan Sugar.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines Saturn, a planet raised in the freezing outer reaches of the solar system that began life as a strange combination of rock and ice. Born outside the snow line, with an abundance of building materials, it soon grew to dwarf Earth, drawing in colossal amounts of the hydrogen and helium that permeated the early solar system. As Nasa's Cassini probe has discovered, the planet remained ring-less most of its life, until a fateful encounter changed everything. Less than a hundred million years ago, one of Saturn's ice moons was drawn too close to the planet. In a truly cataclysmic event, the entire moon was destroyed and the rings were born.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. Here, Fred heads to Marrakesh in the company of TV chef Andi Oliver, whose love of soul food has led to an obsession with the city's rich culinary heritage. The maitre d' reveals he has been to Marrakesh before but has yet to have a truly memorable meal. Can Andi change his mind as they are served lamb and couscous at a legendary family restaurant, chicken tagine for under £10, and breakfast on a spectacular roof terrace?(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, serving up a selection of questions on the subject of restaurants. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Sara Pascoe and Mark Watson.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: With the rest of the team away on international duties, Petey announces he is hosting a barbecue. But when Mattie, Benji and Jack find they are the only guests, a long, strange afternoon unfolds. Meanwhile, Jack's dad oversteps the mark by posting club business on social media and Cesare's job looks uncertain. Have the fans turned against him? Comedy, starring Starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampires attend their first human Super Bowl party, while Guillermo's search for virgins uncovers a deadly threat. Comedy, starring Kayvan Novak, Matt Berry and Harvey Guillen.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampire room-mates fear they have been electronically cursed, leaving Guillermo to thwart a deadly threat to his masters. Comedy, starring Kayvan Novak and Harvey Guillen.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Britain Fed
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Ade Adepitan gain access to some of Britain's biggest supermarkets and their suppliers to see how they are dealing with the coronavirus outbreak, the most testing time in their history. Sara finds out what goes on in one supermarket over 24 hours, from opening to closing and all through the night, to see how they are coping during the crisis. Elsewhere, Ade finds out how the nations' farms, factories and depots have risen to the challenge to produce and supply our supermarkets with food in these exceptional circumstances.(n)

programme.title: Soon Gone: A Windrush Chronicle
programme.desc: In 2011, riots take place outside of Cyrus's house, and dementia forces his memories to fragment, bringing back painful incidents from his early life in England. Monologue, performed by Lenny Henry.(n)

programme.title: Soon Gone: A Windrush Chronicle
programme.desc: The Windrush scandal of enforced repatriations, detentions and denial of provisions has brought the question of Michaela's heritage and belonging into focus. Monologue, performed by Olivia-Mai Barrett.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a selection of memorable moments when the pitchers turned the tables on the panel, including the poker game entrepreneur who forced the dragons to compete against each other for his business. Plus, the inventor of a phone app who provoked the wrath of the panellists and a rejected contestant who went on to become a big commercial success.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable inventions from the show. The programme features a prototype for pedal-powered aviation, a device to stop couples from straying into each other's side of the bed, and the youngest ever entrepreneur to be backed by the Dragon.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the house's fortunes in the 18th and 19th centuries. He begins by investigating the death of owner Joseph Holbrook, and discovering why his daughter was written out of the will. A later resident of the home rises to become mayor in the 1830s, but had to find a way to prevent riots that had ravaged the city years earlier from occurring again. David also delves into the life of a servant who worked in the house in the 1870s, only to leave domestic servitude for an abusive marriage, and discovers how a successful teacher who later owned the house ended up committed to an asylum.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay and his guests Jake Robinson and Sarah Moore demonstrate how to grow lettuce, make a stained-glass window and build a dresser.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Gloucester Antiques Centre, where the red and blue teams go shopping for treasures with experts Kate Bliss and Charles Hanson. Away from the contest, Charlie learns about astronomer William Herschel, who discovered the planet Uranus.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boards one of the world's most famous trains, the Rocky Mountaineer, to cross the backbone of the North American continent from Kamloops to the spa resort of Banff. This magnificent journey takes him to the highest point of the 19th century transcontinental Canadian Pacific Railway line at Kicking Horse Pass, past Lake Louise and inside spiral tunnels blasted through the mountains. Along the way, Michael hears of the harsh and dangerous conditions endured by the Chinese and European labourers who built the railway, and looks back at the historic driving of the Last Spike, which completed the Canadian Pacific Railway in 1885.(n)

programme.title: The Home That Two Built
programme.desc: The documentary tracking 50 years of lifestyle and homemaking programmes on BBC Two concludes by looking at the 1990s and 2000s, when homes were transformed by chucking out the chintz and gardens were turned into outside rooms. Soon there was a craze for decluttering and minimal living, along with a slew of programmes about buying and selling property, homes in the country and living abroad. Featuring contributions by Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Diarmuid Gavin and Charlie Dimmock. Mel Giedroyc narrates.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen head to Monmouthshire, to sample the mouthwatering delights of Abergavenny. The pair are impressed by the variety of locally produced food on offer in the market town known as the gateway to Wales and feast on a Nepalese curry, drink Welsh wine, try making sweets that the miners would have taken down the pit and drop into a Michelin-starred restaurant. They also attend a barbecue at the castle, where they are serenaded by a male voice choir.(n)

programme.title: World Beaters
programme.desc: England defender Lucy tells how she fought back from injury to represent her country.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Germany v Portugal. A chance to see the Euro 2008 quarter-final at St Jakob-Park in Basel, Switzerland.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from Powderham Castle in Devon. Dating back to the 14th century, it is the long standing home of the Courtenay family.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In picturesque Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon. En route, he also visits the home of British mountaineering, Pen y Gwryd, to hear how an Edwardian journalist and poet created a climbing community, which grew to include men who would conquer Everest in the 1950s.(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines the remotest part of the solar system, a place that the most mysterious planets - Uranus, Neptune and dwarf planet Pluto - call home. Barely visible to the naked eye, Uranus was once thought to be the furthest planet from the Sun, before Neptune was discovered. Thanks to a rare alignment of the planets in 1976, Voyager 2 was sent for mankind's only flyby of these ice worlds. Even at such cold temperatures, great storms whip around these frozen worlds, which are home to spectacular moons and intricate ring systems. Then in 2015, Nasa's New Horizons probe pushed the frontier even further into space with its extraordinary passage to the dwarf planet Pluto.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on growing tomatoes and has ideas of how to fill gaps in the garden once the flowers of spring are over. Joe Swift visits the small city garden of an interior designer where some unusual plants have been used to add an element of surprise to the outdoor space, while in Kent Arit Anderson meets a community whose members have all been prescribed gardening as part of their recovery from physical and mental illness.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cameraman Charlie Hamilton James has been captivated by otters for the last 25 years and has filmed them more than anyone else. Now, through the eyes of three river orphaned otters, he sets out to reveal their survival secrets and exactly why he believes these creatures are so special.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools chats to singer, songwriter, broadcaster and frontman of Elbow, Guy Garvey, who has a long history with Late, having performed six times - the first back in 2003, and most recently last year when Elbow released their eighth studio album Giants of All Sizes. Alongside making music, Guy presents a weekly show on BBC 6 Music, so he is no stranger to delving into the back catalogues to bring listeners his favourite music, and he does just that here, illustrated with treats from the Later archive.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Selma (2014)
programme.desc: Although the Civil Rights Act of 1964 legally desegregates the South, discrimination remains rampant in certain areas, making it very difficult for blacks to register to vote. In 1965, amidst a tense atmosphere of unrest, civil rights campaigner Dr Martin Luther King Jr plans a peaceful march across Alabama from Selma to Montgomery. He is pressured to cancel it, but persists as his goal is to persuade President Lyndon B Johnson to change the law, ensuring black people can register and enjoy full democratic rights. Fact-based drama, starring David Oyelowo, Tom Wilkinson, Tim Roth and Carmen Ejogo.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Following the creation of alebrijes, which are brightly coloured, fantastical creatures carved from copal wood and decorated in extremely detailed paintwork.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a secret little loft hidden in the woods, surrounded by evergreens and ruffled, green bushes and grass.(n)

programme.title: Shock and Awe: The Story of Electricity
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili explains how the study of electric fields and electromagnetic waves led to breakthroughs in communications and broadcasting, creating a world more dependent than ever on electrical power. He also investigates why scientists researching electrical superconductivity believe their work could have far-reaching effects.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.sub-title: Blood and Gold: The Making of Spain with Simon Sebag Montefiore
programme.desc: The historian uncovers the truth about Spain's hero El Cid. He also investigates the attack on the city-state of Granada, waged by King Ferdinand and Queen Isabella, the horror of the Spanish Inquisition, and in the process discovers an unsettling story about one of his own ancestors.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world.(n)

programme.title: The High Art of the Low Countries
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon reveals how the entrepreneurial and industrious region of the Low Countries rose to become a cultural leader in the modern age, and despite its small size, managed to produce important forward-thinking artists such as Van Gogh, Delvaux and Magritte. He travels to the remote beach in north-west Holland that inspired Mondrian's transition toward abstract grid painting and examines the psychology and social history of the area.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a secret little loft hidden in the woods, surrounded by evergreens and ruffled, green bushes and grass.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Mexico
programme.desc: Following the creation of alebrijes, which are brightly coloured, fantastical creatures carved from copal wood and decorated in extremely detailed paintwork.(n)

programme.title: Shock and Awe: The Story of Electricity
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili explains how the study of electric fields and electromagnetic waves led to breakthroughs in communications and broadcasting, creating a world more dependent than ever on electrical power. He also investigates why scientists researching electrical superconductivity believe their work could have far-reaching effects.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock asks astronaut Jessica Meir what it was like to take part in the first all-female spacewalk with Christina Koch.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a countryside scene, complete with a big red barn and a rickety wooden fence.(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: Peasant girl Tess Durbeyfield is encouraged by her father to seek advancement with their supposed relatives, the wealthy D'Urbervilles, and meets the charming Alec, man of the house since his father's death. He showers her with attention and gifts, promising her family will want for nothing - but as she soon discovers to her cost, there's a dark side to her persistent new suitor. Thomas Hardy adaptation, starring Gemma Arterton, Hans Matheson, Ian Puleston-Davies and Anna Massey.(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun in Colour
programme.desc: Egyptologist Elizabeth Frood takes viewers back to 1922 to experience the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb as it happened, with the help of colourised photographs and film footage. The documentary follows the story from the first encounter between Howard Carter and Lord Carnarvon, the discovery of a vast chamber filled with ancient relics to the opening of the burial chamber itself, and reveals how the discoveries made changed how the boy king and Egypt as a nation were viewed around the world.(n)

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Michael Scott employs the latest 3D-scanning technology and virtual reality to reveal the historical secrets of Cairo and Ancient Egypt. He explores the first pyramid ever built at Saqqara and finds out how it helped inspire the Great Pyramid of Giza. With access to robbers' tunnels and hidden chambers normally closed to the public, the presenter uses laser scans to help understand how the pyramids were built and shows how the Great Pyramid was designed and constructed. Michael then investigates the Sphinx and tries to determine which pharaoh is represented in the face of the legendary monument.(n)

programme.title: The Man Who Shot Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Margaret Mountford travels to Egypt's Valley of the Kings to investigate the story of British photographer Harry Burton, whose images of the Tutankhamun excavation in the 1920s created a global sensation. In addition to exploring the locations where Burton worked, the presenter also reveals how the images led to a craze in Egyptian designs, and how present-day photographers, such as Harry Cory Wright, are still influenced by his boundary-pushing work today.(n)

programme.title: Fabric of Britain
programme.sub-title: Fabric of Britain: The Story of Embroidery
programme.desc: Plantagenet expert and medievalist Dan Jones concludes his journey by exploring the history of English embroidery, which once held a reputation as the finest in the Western world. He goes in search of surviving pieces from the medieval age and discovers how they were desired by kings and popes throughout the Christian world.(n)

programme.title: Palace for the People
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the hidden secrets within five of communism's most splendid palaces through the eyes of the people who designed, built and worked in them.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a countryside scene, complete with a big red barn and a rickety wooden fence.(n)

programme.title: Tutankhamun in Colour
programme.desc: Egyptologist Elizabeth Frood takes viewers back to 1922 to experience the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb as it happened, with the help of colourised photographs and film footage. The documentary follows the story from the first encounter between Howard Carter and Lord Carnarvon, the discovery of a vast chamber filled with ancient relics to the opening of the burial chamber itself, and reveals how the discoveries made changed how the boy king and Egypt as a nation were viewed around the world.(n)

programme.title: Opera Italia
programme.desc: Conductor Antonio Pappano traces the history of Italian opera, beginning by exploring the work of Monteverdi and Rossini, as well as Handel and Mozart, who were pivotal in the development of the art form. He enlists the help of singers Juan Diego Florez, Joyce DiDonato, Danielle de Niese, Sarah Connolly and Pietro Spagnoli along the way.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 26 October 1989 and featuring performances by Chris Rea, Adeva and Living in a Box.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: With cinemas closed and the nation in lockdown, the UK's most-trusted screen-guides turn their attention from the big screen to the small screen for the first time. Broadcast from Mark and Simon's respective homes, the duo present a guide to the best - and worst - of streaming culture, across movies and premium TV.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Anthea Turner and Andy Crane present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 2 November 1989 and featuring performances by Deborah Harry, Phil Collins and Martika.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by groups and solo artists from the decade, beginning with music by the Kinks, Roxy Music, Elton John and Queen, plus a rendition of The Jean Genie by David Bowie recorded in January 1973.(n)

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from.(n)

programme.title: Fathers and Songs: Music for Father's Day
programme.desc: A selection of songs that celebrate fatherhood and probe the emotional complexities of a man's relationship with his son or daughter. Featured artists of past and present include Kid Creole and the Coconuts, Cat Stevens, Emmylou Harris, Paul Simon, Madonna, Peter Gabriel, Mike and the Mechanics, Suggs and the Blockheads, Neil Young, James Brown, Ozzy and Kelly Osbourne and Pigbag.(n)

programme.title: When Pop Went Epic: The Crazy World of the Concept Album
programme.desc: Rick Wakeman examines the roots of the concept album, a musical format usually based around a structured narrative, though sometimes tied together by a loose theme. He unpacks some of the most ambitious - and ridiculous - projects, from Woody Guthrie's Dustbowl Ballads to Tales from Topographic Oceans by Yes. Other works considered include the Beach Boys' Pet Sounds, George Clinton's Mothership Connection, The Wall by Pink Floyd and the Flaming Lips' Yoshimi Battles the Pink Robots.(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert joins a crew of road workers in south Wales to find out what maintaining the highways and byways is really like. programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Cathy is forced by HR to take compassionate leave and see a bereavement councillor, but she is too scared to tell Tess, so Cat says she should pretend she's going to work but come and hang out at her house instead. Cathy agrees and returns home to find Tess taking over yet more of the wedding planning, making her anxious again. Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Hoveringham overlooks the River Trent, leaving them feeling very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including a furniture restorer who has taken to wearing her husband's clothes, a budding Paralympian who is looking for a grown-up, wheelchair-friendly style and a hip-hop aficionado who wants to upgrade to a smarter street look. Finally, one of the stylists gets to work on a military wife and full-time mum who, after spending half her marriage living on army bases, says she has forgotten what fashion is.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: It's a busy weekend for the medics as thousands of visitors flood into the city for the Grand National weekend - and first up is a visitor falling down some steps at the train station. Mo and Tony are called to a baby that has fallen from its pram, and soon realise the mother is under the influence of alcohol, while Anthony and Mandy are dispatched to a nursing home where a patient is confused state fainting. Paramedics and sisters Danielle and Stacey treat the first casualty at the racecourse - a jockey who has broken his ankle falling from his horse, meaning he will have to miss the big race the following day.(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.sub-title: True North: Big Night at the Bingo
programme.desc: Documentary following the regulars at a West Belfast bingo hall, as they prepare for one of the busiest nights of the year - when the prize money rises to four figures. Among the players are Eilish, who learned the game at her grandmother's knee while in her teens, Margaret, who is juggling her job, her children, a college course, and her husband's health problems, and Kate, who is preparing to celebrate a friend's birthday.(n)

programme.desc: An urgent call is made by a man whose wife is in cardiac arrest, with a doctor and paramedic team from North West Air Ambulance immediately dispatched to assist on the job. Meanwhile out on the road, crewmates Chris and V are dispatched to a category-three call for a patient who's fallen. However, once the patient is safely treated the couple's dogs become the focus of the paramedics' attention. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including a furniture restorer who has taken to wearing her husband's clothes, a budding Paralympian who is looking for a grown-up, wheelchair-friendly style and a hip-hop aficionado who wants to upgrade to a smarter street look. Finally, one of the stylists gets to work on a military wife and full-time mum who, after spending half her marriage living on army bases, says she has forgotten what fashion is.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: It's a busy weekend for the medics as thousands of visitors flood into the city for the Grand National weekend - and first up is a visitor falling down some steps at the train station. Mo and Tony are called to a baby that has fallen from its pram, and soon realise the mother is under the influence of alcohol, while Anthony and Mandy are dispatched to a nursing home where a patient is confused state fainting. Paramedics and sisters Danielle and Stacey treat the first casualty at the racecourse - a jockey who has broken his ankle falling from his horse, meaning he will have to miss the big race the following day.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton assists a couple with a love of entertaining who have £450,000 to find a country home in Leicestershire where they can host family and friends. Alistair also meets the Duchess of Rutland, who tells him all about an exciting discovery at Belvoir Castle.(n)

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country at Home

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Father's Day
programme.desc: Ahead of a Father's Day with a difference his year, the comedian sets up for a day on the road outside Michael's front door. With social distancing taken into account, the two of them will spend some quality time together as best they can, look at some old family photos, awkward home video footage and tell some classic Whitehall tales.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Cathy is forced by HR to take compassionate leave and see a bereavement councillor, but she is too scared to tell Tess, so Cat says she should pretend she's going to work but come and hang out at her house instead. Cathy agrees and returns home to find Tess taking over yet more of the wedding planning, making her anxious again. Comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until their father dies. Starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: With a lot preying on her mind, the last thing Kayleigh needs is to find herself given the task of promoting national blackcurrant jam week. Fortunately, her car-sharing colleague John is on hand to encourage her with plenty of unwanted advice. Wry comedy, co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: George Floyd: A Killing That Shook the World - Panorama
programme.desc: The death of George Floyd in Minneapolis has prompted the biggest protests about race and police brutality in the US for 50 years. Around the world, thousands of people have joined marches against racism. Reporter Clive Myrie asks if this could be a moment that changes race relations in America for good. He hears from protesters, eyewitnesses and former police officers about why this killing has had such a powerful impact, and speaks to people from black and white communities in Minneapolis about their hopes and fears for the future.(n)

programme.title: Am BFG (The BFG) (1989)
programme.desc: A friendly giant befriends a lonely little girl and transports her to a strange land. Unfortunately, a number of the other giants there have acquired a taste for eating children. Determined to stop their nefarious habit, the pair set out to charm the Queen of England into putting her armed forces at their disposal. Animated adventure, based on Roald Dahl's classic tale, with the voices of Donald Smith, Catriona Lexy Campbell and Donna Morrison.(n)

programme.desc: Linda Macleod is joined by Hannah and Ailig to make fishcakes with potatoes and smoked salmon.(n)

programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack meets Sophie and her guinea pigs, Timmy and Max.(n)

programme.title: Na Floogals

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall

programme.desc: Documentary featuring facts about black bears. Beth is a cute bear cub, growing up with her brother under their mother's watchful eye. This is a story of action and adventure that includes eating, getting stuck in trees and taking a stroll in the nearest town. It's also a race for survival, as the little one will needs to have eaten enough to see her through her first hibernation.(n)

programme.desc: Calum French's over-indulgence has not gone un-noticed in Fraochy Bay, though he is blissfully unaware until he hears a knock at the door. An intervention is about to take place and it's certain that poor Calum has never experienced anything like it.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: In an episode curated by Siobhan Miller and Euan Burton, Kathleen MacInnes, Karan Casey, Louis Abbott, Hannah Read, Kris Drever and Olov Johannsson perform.(n)

programme.title: Ameireaga air Film (America in Colour)
programme.desc: The rise of powerful families in the 20th century, including the Rockefellers, the Vanderbilts, the Roosevelts and the Kennedys.(n)

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: An uplifting performance from Sian and their musical friends at Na Trads in 2019.(n)

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads prepare for a family barbecue on the beach, with Stilton burgers, marinated beef and vegetable kebabs, along with salad, Parma Ham potatoes and marinated prawn skewers.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: Kit and Pup explore the opposites Push and Pull.(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: Kit and Pup are exploring the opposites Long and Short.(n)

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: The puffin family is woken through the night by a very strange noise which is revealed to be a frog who can't find his way to the pond after his hibernation. He needn't worry though as the puffins are on hand to help.(n)

programme.title: Botannan Araid Uilleim (William's Wish Wellingtons)
programme.desc: Adventures for younger viewers, with the boy and his magic boots.(n)

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.sub-title: Pàdraig Post agus na Brògan Barraicht' Diet (Postman Pat and the Super Jet Boots)
programme.desc: Seonaidh awaits a special pair of shoes - jet boots that he plans to showcase at the Festival of Flight. However, the boots go an an unexpected detour before delivery.(n)

programme.title: Blàrag a' Bhò (Connie the Cow)
programme.sub-title: Taihbs an t- Sneachda (The Snow Ghost)
programme.desc: While out walking on a winter's day, Blàrag and Paids encounter a strange object moving under the snow, and try to work out what it could be.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Dolina MacLennan reads Cion Fhaclan, written and illustrated by Natalie Russell and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)
programme.desc: Luke doesn't listen to instructions and so loses something of great importance to Eefa. When Otta gets his hands on it, a struggles ensues to get it back.(n)

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Hannah and Niamh from Tain discuss their favourite hobbies, which include gardening, knitting and film making.(n)

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Through her artwork, Maria attempts to convey an important message about ocean pollution.(n)

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: A seagull and a goat get in the way when Stephen Smallberg, who is suffering writer's block, is inspired to put pen to paper.(n)

programme.title: Prosbaig
programme.desc: Donald Morrison looks back at some of the stories which appeared on the 1980s BBC Gaelic current affairs series, Prosbaig. This edition features a look at the issue of domestic violence in the Western Isles, computing being introduced into the school curriculum, and an insight into life in Blairs College in Aberdeen, a training school for students considering a calling to the priesthood, filmed shortly before it closed in 1986.(n)

programme.title: Orain Oir
programme.desc: Delving into the Gaelic music archive to pick out the best performances through the decades.(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor is in Skye to taste a great recipe using shop-bought puff pastry. In Lewis, an old friend shows him the secret to the perfect oatcakes accompanied by a delicious salmon pate.(n)

programme.title: Spors
programme.desc: Derek Mackay and Calum Macaulay provide an insight into the football club, looking at their success on the pitch and what has been going on off it to help make that happen.(n)

programme.title: Spors
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the team, providing an insight into their return to the Premiership.(n)

programme.title: An Bronntanas
programme.desc: Contemporary thriller, following the lives of a local lifeboat crew, set against the backdrop of the Connemara coastline. One stormy night, the men are called out on a rescue mission to an endangered boat on which they find a single person who has been brutally murdered, as well as a cargo of more than one million euros of drugs. They decide to keep the stash for their own financial gain due to the economic depression. However, their lives spiral into a world of paranoia, violence and tragedy.(n)

programme.desc: A special series looking back at Bradley and Barney's previous American adventures. As Bradley and Barney continue their road trip across America they arrive in Texas, where they experience space travel in a centrifuge, before heading to the outskirts of Houston to meet some UFO enthusiasts. Next up, the duo travel to New Orleans in Louisiana, where they meet a Voodoo High Priest for a spot of energy cleansing, before visiting the city's famous French Quarter.(n)

programme.desc: Tiger and Joey take a shine to new girl Amber, while Mateo is the odd choice to run a staff first-aid course. programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.sub-title: Britain's Best Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The expert walker's favourite river walk finds her in Devon, following the green tree canopy of water rapids along the East Lyn and Hoar Oak rivers, where they merge at Watersmeet. While in the county, Julia makes sure she finds time to enjoy a hard-earned cream tea.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla confesses to Peter that Jordan is trying to blackmail her. Adam is pleased to see Daniel having a good time with an attractive woman, and Alya tells Imran how she suspects Sally kicked Geoff out because underneath, she knows Yasmeen is innocent. However, Faye shows her support for Geoff.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Travis has serious school news that he is scared to break to his mum, so Sharon decides to pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. However, her efforts only land the family in more trouble and it is left to Dorien to help out - using her skills of seduction. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Charlie Quirke.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Actor Warwick Davis talks to Piers about his life and career, which includes supporting roles in both the Harry Potter and Star Wars films. The actor and presenter opens up about the struggles he and his wife Sam faced when starting a family and offers an entertaining and revealing account of some of the routine realities of life as a little person, including the challenges of supermarket shopping and airline travel. He also reflects on his leading roles alongside Jennifer Aniston, Val Kilmer and Ricky Gervais, and talks about his work to support other people with dwarfism.(n)

programme.desc: The expert walker's favourite river walk finds her in Devon, following the green tree canopy of water rapids along the East Lyn and Hoar Oak rivers, where they merge at Watersmeet. While in the county, Julia makes sure she finds time to enjoy a hard-earned cream tea.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla confesses to Peter that Jordan is trying to blackmail her. Adam is pleased to see Daniel having a good time with an attractive woman, and Alya tells Imran how she suspects Sally kicked Geoff out because underneath, she knows Yasmeen is innocent. However, Faye shows her support for Geoff.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Travis has serious school news that he is scared to break to his mum, so Sharon decides to pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. However, her efforts only land the family in more trouble and it is left to Dorien to help out - using her skills of seduction. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Charlie Quirke.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill opens his diary to look at a typical year in the life of a You've Been Framed! star, taking in all the major events in the calendar, from Pancake Day disasters to horrific Halloweens.(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Leeds to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: A situation arises when Jonah refuses to sell guns in the store, while Glenn attempts to buy the full stock of morning-after pills.(n)

programme.desc: David from London picks three blind dates from five potential partners, based entirely on the menus they have created. Having dined with his three suitors, he decides which of the men he wants to see again. Narrated by Charlotte Hudson.(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring accidents involving basketball, babies, buckets of water and bottles of Prosecco.(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Johnny Vegas, Shaun Wallace and Brooke Vincent to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois thinks Stewie is trying to kill her because he is not spending enough time with his father. She tells Peter to make an effort to bond with the youngster - but a theme park outing ends in disaster.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois discovers that she has an estranged brother, and combs the country in search of him, eventually finding him in a psychiatric hospital. Meanwhile, Peter starts a society for fat men. Animation, featuring the guest voice of Robert Downey Jr.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger releases photographs of himself to the media, prompting the CIA to form an Alien Task Force to deal with the situation. Meanwhile, Francine gets to grips with being left-handed.(n)

programme.desc: Francine becomes a surrogate mother for gay neighbours Terry and Greg, prompting an outraged Stan to kidnap the baby once she is born. Meanwhile, Steve and Roger think Klaus is out to get them.(n)

programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson.(n)

Thomas's future appears to be uncertain, but he may have found an unexpected ally in Bates.(n)

programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.desc: Since calling off his marriage to Louisa, Martin has begun to question what keeps him in Cornwall and realises that if he is ever to escape, he must conquer the blood phobia that ended his surgical career. Encouraged by aunt Joan's friend Barbara he tackles his fear, but after an accident the pair end up in hospital, where he bumps into his girlfriend from medical school. Later, the return of a familiar face shakes him up even more. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Stephanie Cole and Lia Williams.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good.(n)

Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence.(n)

programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict.(n)

But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan encounters a former nemesis at a party, who drunkenly admits involvement in an earlier robbery in which he was let go due to lack of evidence. programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels from the west of Morocco to the east, heading into the desert on a mission to find out whether a railway line once crossed the Sahara all the way to Timbuktu in Mali. He begins in Marrakesh, taking in Casablanca and Fez before visiting a town that he is convinced has been named in his honour.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An insight into the working life of officers patrolling the nation's roads, using modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice. Presented by Jamie Theakston. After an hour-long chase the team use a stinger to stop a motorbike. In Harrogate, a hostile crowd gathers when a suspect runs into a housing estate.(n)

programme.title: The Railway 24/7
programme.desc: The network is severely disrupted by the 80mph winds of Storm Ciara, leaving lines blocked by fallen trees and stranded passengers in need of a new route home. British transport police officers Tom and Chris take on a demanding weekend night shift, and have to deal with a group of young men who refuse to leave Stockport station. Conductor Jess dreads the arrival of a crowd of football fans - but they turn out to be a much more welcome arrival than expected.(n)

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A man who fell through a window has a phobia of needles, which leads him to refuse any kind of pain relief and even attempt to discharge himself before his injuries are treated. An aggressive patient is brought in by four police officers after smashing up his mother's home, and has to undergo a toxicology test. Dr Audrey Fox tries to save the life of a patient who has overdosed, and a woman suffers a head injury after slipping on a slice of pizza her husband had left on the floor for their dog to eat.(n)

programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. A team of one hundred officers prepare to strike with a series of simultaneous dawn raids, and police in south Wales identify a target couriering drugs around the UK.(n)

programme.title: Bouncers

Senior bouncers Gaz and Pete are helping nightclub door staff deal with a man who has been refused entry - but what should have been a simple job turns out to be a very challenging situation. Tay and Kev remove an elderly man who is threatening customers and abusing staff at a pub.(n)

As the event gets under way, the museum's enthusiastic volunteers dress up in costume to scare the public on the ghost train, while retired minister David entertains the passengers on the journey.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell explores the history of the design and construction of the Tyne Bridge, which links Newcastle and Gateshead, discovering many believe that it was the inspiration for the Sydney Harbour Bridge. He also finds out why the bridge is so revered by Geordies, not just as a way to cross the river, but because it was built to keep shipbuilders employed and their skills alive during the dark days of the 1920s recession.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow examines the work of Italian Renaissance painter Raffaello Santi, known better simply as Raphael, who was born in 1483 and achieved so much in his 37-year life that he was considered equal to Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo. He was highly regarded for his many studies of the Madonna and Child, and artists adopted his idealised forms as the model of beauty until the end of the 19th century.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.sub-title: Yorkshire: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Cameras follow each season in the lives of wildlife in the Yorkshire Dales and on the North York Moors National Parks, beginning with spring. Life is starting to return to the region at a time when the local animals must find food, as well as a mate and raise a family.(n)

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary about Royal Navy destroyer HMS Duncan, following the ups and downs of everyday life for its crew of 280 and capturing dramatic moments in its deployment.(n)

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel.(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Ritz ranks high among London's most legendary hotels, one that has been synonymous with luxury and wealth, as well as being a bastion of privilege for over 100 years. Situated on Piccadilly, just a stone's throw from Buckingham Palace, it has been a home from home for the royal family since the reign of Edward VII. The Ritz has redefined service in luxury hotels, introducing the now-universal concept of the customer always being right. This programme examines its heritage, its famous afternoon teas and the scandalous history of its founder, Cesar Ritz.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Ever Celeb Embarrassments
programme.desc: Part one of two. Narrated by comedian Chris Ramsey, this collection of quick-fire laughs showcases stars who have ended up with egg on their faces. Red-faced celebrities include Lady Gaga, Will Smith, Rihanna, Beyonce, Justin Bieber and Pink, as well as home-grown favourites Phillip Schofield, Holly Willoughby, Gino D'Acampo, Rylan Clark-Neal and Gemma Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi tries to reverse the fortunes of Warwick Hall in Cumbria, one of her grandest hotels yet, but one suffering from low occupancy and negative feedback. While Alex is initially impressed with what she finds, she soon begins to discover the cracks and realises that she has a few harsh home truths to deliver - can she convince the owners that they need to rebrand themselves as a B&B instead of a hotel?(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: For 17 years from 1981 to 1998, the Humber Bridge was the longest single-span suspension bridge in the world, and many consider it to be Britain's most beautiful. Rob Bell discovers the facts about its groundbreaking construction, why it took so long to build and how it changed the lives of the people of Hull and North Lincolnshire.(n)

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow reveals how the Renaissance movement found a new centre in Venice and examines the life and work of Tiziano Vecellio, known to English-speakers as Titian, who pioneered the use of oils with a radical approach to light, colour and brush strokes. His talent was recognised across Europe and he painted portraits of some of the 16th century's most powerful figures, including Pope Paul III and Charles V of Spain.(n)
