programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Craig Charles assesses Samsung's much-anticipated folding Galaxy Z Flip, while Jon Bentley gives Gogglebox star Sandra Martin three affordable 65-inch televisions to check out. Ortis Deley spends the day with his three-year-old daughter at London's annual Toy Fair where they explore the hottest techy playthings on offer for 2020. The team also revisits 1971, a year when floppy disks were not only a thing but were actually floppy and Craig continues his attempt to turn a house into a smart home. Plus, Harry Wallop has his latest tips on how to save money using tech.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a train journey across Sri Lanka along one of the world's best-preserved Victorian railways. The trip begins in the coastal city of Colombo and moves on to the country's cultural and spiritual heart in Kandy. The journey continues through a a very English town that produces some of the world's finest teas, coming to a stop at a hill station surrounded by tropical forests close to one of the world's most spectacular viaducts - the Nine Arch Bridge.(n)

programme.title: A Greek Odyssey with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: Bettany visits the sacred isle of Delos, where it has been forbidden for anyone to permanently reside since ancient times. Wandering the ruins, Bettany discovers a dark history to this incredible site as she explores how the Romans turned Delos into a marketplace for their slave trade. She then moves on to Ikaria, an island famous for its long-lived population, and gets a lesson in wine-making. As she moves on to Mykonos, Bettany's boat is caught in a storm reaching a 10 on the Beaufort scale.(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.sub-title: Most Shocking Talent Show Moments Ever!
programme.desc: Kate Thornton introduces a countdown of disastrous moments from TV talent shows, featuring first-hand contributions from stars including Pete Waterman, Arlene Phillips, Brenda Edwards and Nicola Ward, the Popstars: The Rivals contestant who walked away from the chance to be in Girls Aloud. Plus, Marie Osmond fainting on air in Dancing with the Stars and the Dancing on Ice contestant who nearly got a skate in the eye.(n)

programme.title: El Dorado (1966)
programme.desc: An ageing gunfighter is drawn into the conflict between a land-grabbing cattle baron and a ranching family, but gets wounded in the process. A year later, he returns to town to find the situation has not improved - so along with his sidekick and the drunken town sheriff, he fights to defend the family from its enemies. Western, starring John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, James Caan and Ed Asner.(n)

programme.title: Unbroken Interview Special
programme.desc: Angelina Jolie discusses making her directorial debut with the fact-based Second World War drama, and Jack O'Connell talks about his role in the film.(n)

programme.title: Houseboat (1958)
programme.desc: An Italian socialite rebels against her domineering father and gets a job as a maid for a widowed father. While helping her employer learn to understand his three precocious children, she also falls in love with him and must decide whether or not to reveal her secret. Romantic comedy, starring Cary Grant and Sophia Loren.(n)

programme.title: Unbroken Interview Special
programme.desc: Angelina Jolie discusses making her directorial debut with the fact-based Second World War drama, and Jack O'Connell talks about his role in the film.(n)

programme.title: The Odessa File (1974)
programme.desc: The detailed diaries of a former inmate of a concentration camp fall into the hands of an investigative journalist. Incensed by what he reads, he embarks on a crusade to discover the prison commandant's new identity and expose his crimes to the world. In the process, the trail leads him to a secret organisation that helped Nazi SS officers escape justice. Thriller, based on Frederick Forsyth's novel, starring Jon Voight, Derek Jacobi and siblings Maria and Maximilian Schell.(n)

programme.title: Hampstead (2017)
programme.desc: An American widow finds unexpected love with a man living wild on Hampstead Heath when they take on the developers who want to destroy his home. Romantic comedy drama, starring Brendan Gleeson, Diane Keaton and James Norton.(n)

programme.title: Unbroken Interview Special
programme.desc: Angelina Jolie discusses making her directorial debut with the fact-based Second World War drama, and Jack O'Connell talks about his role in the film.(n)

programme.title: Lucy (2014)
programme.desc: A courier is kidnapped and forced to become a drug mule. When she is exposed to the experimental chemicals she is smuggling, it boosts her intelligence and reflexes to superhuman levels. She uses her newfound abilities to get revenge on her captors, but as the process continues, she evolves into something more than human. Sci-fi thriller, starring Scarlett Johansson and Morgan Freeman.(n)

programme.title: Popstar: Never Stop Never Stopping (2016)
programme.desc: When it becomes obvious that his solo album is a flop, a former boy band member does everything he can to maintain his celebrity status. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg, Jorma Taccone and Akiva Schaffer.(n)

programme.title: Cuban Fury (2014)
programme.desc: A boy's dream of being a champion salsa dancer is shattered by bullies. Twenty years later, he returns to the dance floor as an overweight and under-confident adult, and tries to get his old footwork back in a bid to sweep his boss off her feet. Comedy, starring Nick Frost, Chris O'Dowd, Rashida Jones, Ian McShane and Olivia Colman.(n)

programme.title: Fun Size (2012)
programme.desc: A teenage girl looking forward to a Halloween party has her hopes dashed when her mother insists she take her little brother trick-or-treating instead. Over the course of the night, she loses the youngster, and she and her friends embark on a bizarre journey to find him before anyone notices he is missing. Comedy, starring Victoria Justice and Jackson Nicoll.(n)

programme.title: Decision at Sundown (1957)
programme.desc: A gunslinger rides into a backwoods town intent on finding his dead wife's former lover and exacting revenge for her death. The man he blames now has total control of the town and has the sheriff at his beck and call. Undeterred, the drifter offers his enemy an ultimatum - he has until sundown to leave the town or face retribution for his crimes. Western, with Randolph Scott, John Carroll and Andrew Duggan.(n)

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball.(n)

programme.title: The Court-Martial of Billy Mitchell (1955)
programme.desc: In the 1920s, an American colonel accuses the war department of criminal negligence for denying the air force the funding it needs - but the powers-that-be still regard aircraft as an irrelevant part of the military, and respond by putting him on trial. Courtroom drama, starring Gary Cooper and Rod Steiger, with Ralph Bellamy and Elizabeth Montgomery.(n)

programme.title: The Choir (2014)
programme.desc: A troubled young orphan is sent to an elite choral academy following the death of his single mother. He rebels against its rules, but the school's choirmaster is determined to help him deal with his anger by nurturing his unique musical talents. Drama, starring Dustin Hoffman, Kathy Bates, Debra Winger, Eddie Izzard and Garrett Wareing.(n)

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (2014)
programme.desc: A reporter discovers four humanoid amphibians who live in the sewers of New York, and use their martial arts skills to fight crime. She helps them as they face their toughest challenge yet - defeating a sinister criminal organisation that seems to have power over the entire city. Fantasy adventure, starring Megan Fox and William Fichtner, with the voice of Johnny Knoxville.(n)

programme.title: Unbroken (2014)
programme.desc: An Olympic athlete enlists in the US Air Force during the Second World War. When his plane is shot down, he and two crewmates survive for 33 days lost at sea. Worse is to come when they are captured by Japanese forces, and he endures brutal treatment in a PoW camp run by an infamous war criminal. Fact-based Second World War drama, directed by Angelina Jolie and starring Jack O'Connell and Domhnall Gleeson.(n)

programme.title: St Vincent (2014)
programme.desc: A lonely 12-year-old boy gets locked out of his house, so turns to his next-door neighbour, a misanthropic Vietnam veteran with a gambling addiction. The neighbour takes a shine to the youngster, and offers his services as a babysitter, providing valuable life lessons and an introduction to the joys of gambling, fighting and petty theft. Comedy drama, starring Bill Murray, Jaeden Lieberher, Melissa McCarthy and Naomi Watts.(n)

programme.title: Precinct Seven Five (2014)
programme.desc: Documentary about a corrupt police officer in 1980s New York. In need of money, he began stealing and extorting money and goods from the drug dealers he was supposed to be arresting, and using the narcotics he stole to set up his own criminal enterprise, When he was finally brought to justice in 1992, his arrest exposed widespread corruption on the force.(n)

programme.title: Dakota (1945)
programme.desc: A professional gambler and a railway tycoon's daughter elope, hoping to make their fortune by buying land in Dakota. However, they instead find themselves helping the local farmers defend their homes against unscrupulous businessmen trying to drive them off their land. Western, starring John Wayne and Vera Hruba Ralston.(n)

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri (1954)
programme.desc: An American naval reserve pilot is called up for service in the Korean War, and must leave his family and civilian job as a lawyer. In command of a corps of naval pilots, he heads a bombing campaign on strategic Communist positions, but later comes to question the morality of the conflict. Adventure, starring William Holden, Grace Kelly, Fredric March and Mickey Rooney.(n)

programme.title: The Tin Star (1957)

programme.desc: Mel invites a TV reporter to do a story on her and the family to help improve her public image. However, when the journalist discovers she has a male nanny working for her, she decides to change the focus of the piece. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.sub-title: Dancing with the Stars of Toledo
programme.desc: Mel agrees to participate in a charity dance event to raise money for the children whom Lennox tutors. However, creative differences cause her and Joe to part ways, so Stephanie manages to persuade celebrity dancer Mark Ballas to be Mel's partner. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley realises she has to tell Danny she has feelings for him before his relationship with Robyn gets more serious. Tucker is unhappy with his love life, so Ben and Bonnie invent a secret admirer to give him some much needed confidence. However, the plot goes awry when they use a random photo of a model and Tucker encounters her by chance in the street.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.sub-title: Home Is Where the Wheeler Is
programme.desc: Riley cannot face the mess she has made out of her relationships with Ben and Danny, so she runs home to her mother's house in New Jersey to hide from her problems. When her next door neighbour Evelyn dies, Bonnie plots for the chance to sell her apartment.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Amy are forced to take an unexpected road trip to Arizona to pick up Sheldon after his epic railway journey ends in chaos. Meanwhile, Howard finds Stuart's relationship with his mother strange, and Penny has an interview for a sales job at Bernadette's workplace. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to teach a course at the university, and Howard surprises the gang when he decides to enrol on it. Meanwhile, Amy plays Penny and Bernadette off against each other while the tension brews over Penny's new job. Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly intervenes when she finds out that Coach Mellor has a strained relationship with his brother, Coach Nick, but when the two men face off in the gym, Adam and Barry join in. Meanwhile, Geoff and Erica struggle with their long-distance relationship.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly decides to reinvent herself by going to night school so that she can quit her substitute teacher role and own her own business, but Murray dislikes change and tries to convince her to take a different path. Meanwhile, Barry embarks on a makeover of his own, and Erica struggles to balance her academic studies with her social life in college and calls on Adam for assistance.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: After Captain Holt is transferred to the NYPD Department of Public Relations, the arrival of a new captain causes the precinct to dissolve into chaos - despite the best efforts of Sergeant Jeffords. meanwhile, Peralta and Santiago face the aftermath of their kiss. Kyra Sedgwick and Bill Hader guest star.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The division incurs another personnel change that threatens to put Jake and Amy's budding relationship at risk. Meanwhile, Terry assists Holt with an important public relations matter and Rosa and Gina try to convince Charles to rethink his affection for a fellow officer. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard and Bernadette's life becomes complicated when Stuart starts bringing his new girlfriend home. Penny and Leonard talk about starting a family, and Raj further explores the possibility of an arranged marriage.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Sheldon refuses to tell anyone why he's never mentioned Tam, apparently his childhood best friend, Leonard tries to find out more. Penny and Bernadette invite Raj's fiancee Anu out for dinner so they can find out more about her.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox needs Mel's permission after she is asked about becoming a model, and Mel is not keen until she finds out about the money that could be made. Meanwhile, Ryder has problems approaching a new girl and Joe tries to give him advice. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox and Ryder work on a documentary project together. Meanwhile, Mel plans a lesbian wedding for her cousin on a limited budget, but one of the brides-to-be gets distracted. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.sub-title: It's a Nice Day for a Wheeler Wedding
programme.desc: Bonnie's friendly foe Charlotte helps her prepare for the wedding - but fears arise she may have other plans up her sleeve. Comedy, guest starring Reba McEntire, with Melissa Peterman and Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley faints after Danny asks her to marry him, and when she wakes up, she has no memory of his proposal. Trying to move on from Riley, Ben straight away hits it off with new neighbour Zoey. However, Ben is shocked when their first date reveals he and Zoey have more in common than he thought. Bonnie is determined to finally marry Brad after many aborted attempts, and believing Ben and Danny are to blame, enlists Tucker to help her tie the knot without her sons' knowledge.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle and Genevieve want to take their relationship to a new level, but his ex-wife disrupts their plans. Santiago asks Jeffords to write a letter of recommendation for her, while Holt and Diaz enlist Gina's assistance with a difficult interrogation. Andy Samberg stars.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt and Peralta contract mumps during an investigation, so the pair elect to be quarantined together in the hope that they can use the time to solve the case. Diaz tries to help Boyle through the mourning process for his dog, and Jeffords is forced to clean up Hitchcock and Scully's mess. Oscar Nunez guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Murray fall out over Adam's plans to pursue a comedy career after his attempts to be funny in woodwork class nearly result in his teacher getting injured. Beverly and Geoff worry that college is turning Erica into a pretentious snob, but they allow Barry to coach them in the finer points of culture in an attempt to stay close to her.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry looks to secure a spot on the high school football team but is quickly consigned to the bench by Coach Mellor for his lack of sporting prowess. Adam decides to help his brother understand that there are other ways he can contribute to the squad's success.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy are thrilled when their super-asymmetry theory is proved correct by two physicists from Chicago - until the experts try to exclude Amy from their Nobel Prize nomination. Bernadette experiences a big success at work and plans for Penny to become a part of it - whether she wants to or not.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is excited to help Howard and Bernadette navigate some bureaucratic paperwork, until he discovers that they are breaking the law, leaving him with a difficult choice between following the rules or staying loyal to his friends. Leonard is disappointed when he is excluded from a scientific project his friends are working on.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks Favourites
programme.desc: Looking back at John Paul and Craig's romance. Sarah's engagement party comes to an abrupt halt when she finds Craig in bed with John Paul.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: While investigating a string of hotel robberies together, Peralta is upset to discover Santiago is considering leaving the precinct to work with his rival, the Vulture, in the special crimes unit. Holt and Jeffords try to think up some strategies to make the team more efficient. Featuring a guest appearance by Adam Sandler.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: An argument between Leonard and Sheldon over the ending of a Harry Potter book affects Amy and Penny's living arrangements. Meanwhile, Howard goes on a trip to Las Vegas with Bernadette and asks Raj to keep an eye on his mother while he is away.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny celebrate Valentine's Day, but their evening takes a turn for the worse when the latter's ex-boyfriend proposes to his girlfriend in the same restaurant. Meanwhile, Sheldon enlists the help of his assistant Alex to find the perfect gift for Amy.(n)

programme.title: Terminator Genisys (2015)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting in a post-apocalyptic war against living machines is sent back in time to prevent an attempt to change the past - but when he arrives, he finds that history has been changed beyond recognition - and that a plot is being hatched by an unexpected enemy to destroy the future of the human race. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger and Emilia Clarke.(n)

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: A new power arrives in Sunnydale's underworld in the shape of thrill-seeking cockney vampire Spike and his insane lover Drusilla. Spike has killed two slayers in the past and is keen to add Buffy to the list, launching an all-out attack on the high school while the chosen one is in the midst of organising parent teacher night. Supernatural adventure, with Sarah Michelle Gellar and James Marsters.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Devon County Show, where she discovers the secret of home-made handscrub and learns about beekeeping and making beeswax candles. She also tries her hand at crafting jewellery in silver clay - an ideal way of making unusual but inexpensive gifts.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Lee and Lynn Fox from South Yorkshire have been regular visitors to the Tuscan city of Lucca since Lee's job as a mechanical engineer first took him there over 10 years ago. Lynn's hairdressing business and Lee's job keep the couple very busy, so making the most of their quality time together is key. Laura Hamilton makes it her mission to show them a variety of properties as close to the city centre as possible, on a budget of £170,000.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a Yorkshire textile mill, where Simon repurposes a mill trolley into a coffee table and Henry struggles to repair a Soviet-era motorbike.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Surrey and Hampshire. Simon builds a huge kitchen table from discarded rafters and gives a 1970s campervan a new lease of life. Henry hankers after an American tractor and Royal Enfield motorbike and sidecar.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Andrew and Vicki Dodden from Cannington, Somerset, who plan to transform an 18th-century cottage into an eco-friendly home that is suitable for modern living. The couple stand to save £150,000 by opting to self-build, and visit a nearby house in Wells for inspiration on how they can tweak the design of their conversion to get the very best results.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Wisteria House, Hampshire, where owner Jane Faulkner invites guests to her establishment. Later, the group members try their hands at milking a cow, while 25-year-old Zak causes surprises when he reveals his years of hospitality experience.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit The Living Room B&B in Bournemouth on their second night, which is co-owned by 25-year-old Zak Busby-Pelekanou. While at the establishment, Mike and Charlie have a hairy issue, and Jane and Amy encounter a spider, before getting to grips with an ambitious breakfast menu.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Valley Hotel in Anglesey, where owner Mike Lewis hopes his five-star pedigree will help him reap dividends in the competition. After a visit to a local RNLI station, guests Jane and Amy are left feeling sluggish when they spot an unwanted visitor in their room, and their criticism leaves the hosts looking for answers.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Elvira Barratt welcome the contestants to Barratt's at Ty'n Rhyl, Denbighshire, but the group has an unpleasant experience on arrival. The guests take part in an activity at a local cookery school where they are taught the art of preparing and cooking sheep's testicles. However, come feedback time, the hosts are left feeling distraught.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet to find out who earned the most and have it out with their rivals. Jane and Amy kick off proceedings with a gripe over David and Elvira's coffee criticism, yet are confronted themselves by Zak, who has a score to settle. Mike and Charlie of the Valley Hotel want to get to the bottom of an unwanted intruder during their stay at Wisteria House, and the ladies are challenged again regarding their cleanliness comments. Emotions run high as the cash is counted and the winner is revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team rummage through the hangar of a Vulcan bomber, where Henry finds a cockpit seat to restore, and Simon attempts to turn part of a jet engine into a coffee table.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: John and William Dawson must gather in 2,000 sheep across a 3,500-acre expanse of the Dales, while in the Lakes, Trudy Harrison tries to turn round the village of Bootle's fortunes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree await the arrival of their guests, who are expecting a luxurious Christmas at the chateau. Dick continues work on installing the lift, but the project tests his ingenuity to the limit, while Angel finds more valuables in the attic, including a collection of vinyl records.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Robert and Milla Gaukroger, as they try to add three extensions to a 1980s property overlooking Windermere in the Lake District. Robert has designed his own heating system to achieve maximum sustainability, but the project begins with only £100,000 of the £400,000 budget in place, and he and his family have to move into the existing building while work goes on around them.(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Lost Pyramid
programme.desc: Documentary following archaeologists working on a 4,000-year-old pyramid containing a burial chamber, still apparently sealed. As light enters the tomb for the first time, the artefacts contained within reveal a gripping detective story as although there are no signs of entry, the contents have been disturbed. To solve the mystery, Egyptologists explore the nearby Black Pyramid to see how pyramid builders used every trick in the book to protect the treasures, discovering a dark and complex labyrinth of dummy tombs, false passages and stone portcullises to fool robbers.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cyclist Stephen, who's 48, has severely damaged his ankle after coming off his bike deep in woodland in Surrey, and doctors treat a man rushed in after collapsing with a suspected stroke in his garden. Meanwhile, Bridget, who's 70, broke her wrist when she slipped on a mountain path in Greece.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gary, 50, is brought into St George's hospital by ambulance after a garage collapsed and fell on his legs. His wife Peta joins him, while Maggie, 83, comes in to A&E with suspected sepsis. She's joined by her son and daughter, who also deal with the fact their mother has lung cancer. Plus, cameras follow one-year-old George, who is rushed in with his mother after drinking nail varnish remover.(n)

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley host as contestants pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers. Barry McGuigan is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, where he meets a stubborn owner who doesn't react well to criticism. Tom bought the restaurant for his daughter Andrea, but six years later he's still refusing to let her run it. Can the chef break Tom's iron grip on the business and persuade him to change his outdated approach?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Jonnie shows them how far their budget will stretch in this highly desirable corner of the US.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Ian and Steph Shaw, who plan to build an oak-framed cottage just outside Horsham, West Sussex. They hope to construct the house for around £100,000 less than it would cost to buy, so visit similar structures in the area for ideas, inspiration and advice.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Who'd a Thought It in Grafty Green, Kent, where partners Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin hope to impress their fellow guests. However, Matthew and Liam are underwhelmed by the cleanliness levels.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the New Osborne Hotel in Blackpool on their second night, where proprietor Matthew Bracegirdle and entertainment manager Liam hope their guests feel at home in their family friendly establishment. However, Jackie and Paul are unimpressed by their small room, and Joe and Alex find the cleanliness levels lacking.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Fairy Falls Hotel in Trefriw, Conwy County Borough, where B&B newcomers Jackie and Paul Langshaw hope to impress the other contestants. They take their guests to a local maze in the afternoon and, after an uncomfortable night's sleep for Alex, some of the breakfast orders are misplaced.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Husband and wife Liz and Brian Jardine welcome their guests to The Great Trewern B&B, Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where they enjoy the quiet life. Joe and Alex feel far away from it all, but the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress the other guests. At dinner, talk turns to first impressions and it appears it is all water under the bridge for Jackie and Joe, but she remains upset with the feedback Matthew and Liam gave her.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one final time to find out what they have been paid. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole builds a steampunk lamp from a copper electric central heating pipe and Simon O'Brien modernises a dated Welsh dresser during visits to Whitby and Shipley in Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: In Swaledale, mountain rescuer Pete Roe is called to Tan Hill Inn, Britain's highest pub, where a man has gone missing in freezing overnight temperatures.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Two-and-a-half years have passed since Dick and Angel Strawbridge swapped their flat in Southend for a 19th-century chateau in France costing only £280,000, and in that time, the pair have worked to transform the derelict property into a fairytale castle. However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits a project undertaken by Daren Howarth and Adi Nortje, a couple who moved from Brighton to Brittany to create an earth-sheltered house built almost entirely from recycled materials. Their aim was to enlist the help of unskilled volunteers to work on the home, in exchange for teaching them construction techniques to keep down the costs. The presenter returns to learn how their experiment turned out.(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Brianna travels through time at Craigh Na Dun to intervene in her parents' death at Fraser's Ridge. Fantasy period drama, starring Sophie Skelton.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted get involved in yet another bout of rivalry with their counterparts over on Rugged Island - this time by writing a song that will allow them the dubious honour of representing Ireland in the Eurovision-style music contest Eurosong. Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan descends on Craggy Island to investigate rumours that Father Jack has developed the unfortunate habit of sleepwalking in the nude - only to come face to face with his worst fear. Clerical comedy, with Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer hosts the words-and-numbers game, with assistance from Rachel Riley, while poet Pam Ayres comes up with a verse or two as she joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.(n)

The staff at St George's also have to deal with 48-year-old Tony, who was riding pillion with his father when their motorbike was involved in a crash, leaving them both badly injured, with Tony having to be flown in to the hospital by emergency helicopter.(n)

However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Josh and Rhian Cooper, who plan to build a stylish home on the cliffs overlooking the seaside town of Aberdovey, on the coast of Gwynedd. The presenter hopes to make them a saving of £60,000 by designing the master bedroom, and the couple decide to visit another coastal home in Fishguard for extra inspiration.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit is to the 12th-century Oxenham Arms, Devon, where owners Simon and Lyn Powell hope to kick-off the competition in style. The guests participate in broom dancing and after throwing some moves, the hosts have fellow contestant John pegged as the one to watch.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Elegant House bed and breakfast in Liverpool on their second night, where host John Brady hopes his unique property will stand out from the crowd. Nikki and David get to grips with the unconventional facilities, and David reveals his background as a hotel inspector - with Amanda making her opinions on the star rating system very clear.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to Monmouthshire, where Peter and Amanda Copp want to rope their guests into the world of 'glamping' at Hidden Valley Yurts. John has a hairy encounter in his yurt, and the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress David and Nikki. The next morning, the DIY breakfast proves underwhelming for some, while the hosts discover that the great outdoors is not everyone's cup of tea.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former hotel inspector and owner David Stanbridge and manager Nikki Deans welcome the contestants to the Cley Hall Hotel in Spalding, Lincolnshire. Peter and Amanda are impressed by their room, but Simon and Lyn are unhappy with the state of the carpet. The next morning, the guests decide to put their eight-minute breakfast rule to the test, and some of them have concerns about Nikki's waitressing skills.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and confront their critics. Simon and Lyn kick off proceedings by demanding answers from John about his disturbed night's sleep, but then find themselves taken to task by David over their cleanliness feedback. However, the former hotel inspector receives his own interrogation from Peter and Amanda, who want to know why he didn't praise their shared bathroom facilities. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.(n)

programme.desc: In Grasmere, chef Kevin Tickle goes foraging to cook some local dishes at his Forest Side restaurant, and in Wensleydale, gamekeeper David Hutchinson readies the estate for a shoot.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits carpenter Bill Bradley and his wife Sarah, who set out to build two timber houses on a very limited space on the site of an old wood workshop in south London. The presenter finds out whether their dream of a mortgage-free life came true despite the complications posed by the awkward location, and a soaring budget.(n)

programme.title: Code 37: Sex Crimes
programme.desc: Part two of two. After Hannah and Bob discover that Vincent Manteau is Roos's uncle, the case becomes personal, particularly for Hannah, because she knows what damage family secrets can cause. Roos' young sister Phaedra is in trouble and asks for help, which leads Hannah to suspect that anyone close to Roos is a target. In Flemish.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary following patients being treated at St George's in south-west London reaches its 200th edition. Ky, 18, is brought in after being knocked off her motorbike in a hit-and-run, and her mum Sam is by her side as doctors assess the damage to her legs and pelvis. Aleks, 41, is suffering from confusion and memory loss after falling from her loft onto her head, and as her husband Paulo arrives at the hospital, medical staff are still unsure as to whether she has sustained permanent brain damage.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to discover if they can go motor racing for less than it costs to play golf. There's also a double helping of guests, with rock star Slash and Formula One driver Kimi Raikkonen taking to the track in Reasonably Priced Cars. Richard also gets behind the wheel of the BMW M5, which is powered by a twin-turbo V8 engine instead of the high-revving V10 of its predecessor.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James concludes his examination of the social significance of cars by exploring how aspiration and new wealth were behind the development of some of the greatest models ever made. He tries to make sense of the baffling world of company car hierarchy by holding a travelling salesman race-off and indulges his 1980s urges with the twin delights of a Lamborghini and a Porsche. He also unveils his choice of the ultimate people's car - a vehicle that can lay claim to being the greatest in history.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. James focuses on passenger comfort, Richard tries to shorten response times by getting other drivers out of the way more effectively, an issue Jeremy tackles with the invention of what he calls the 'sports ambulance'. Their three very different creations are then put to the test with a series of severe and often messy challenges. Plus, Formula One driver Daniel Ricciardo is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M4 at the track, before switching to the German manufacturer's hybrid-powered i8 for a trip up the A1 to Whitby, North Yorkshire, where he decides which of the two he would rather take for the drive home. With Land Rover production ceasing in the UK later this year, Richard Hammond pays tribute to the 4x4 by attempting to drive one straight up the side of a dam, while James May puts the Mercedes AMG GT through its paces. Taking to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car are Will Smith and Margot Robbie.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets to grips with a Peel P50, the smallest car to go into production, and finds a surprising number of uses for it. James May experiences the luxury of the Rolls-Royce Phantom Drophead Coupe convertible, and the Stig puts the Ferrari 599 GTB Fiorano through its paces on the test track. The Rolling Stones' Ronnie Wood is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People

He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May encounter artillery shells, corsets and classic hot hatchbacks as they take a nostalgic look at French company Peugeot, while Richard Hammond is at the test track to compare the Porsche Cayman GTS with the latest Chevrolet Corvette. James also takes the 950bhp LaFerrari hybrid hypercar out for a spin in Italy, and singer Olly Murs is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May find the fastest way to get around London - bike, boat or car - in a race through the capital during rush hour. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson test-drives two estate cars - the BMW M5 and Mercedes E63. Richard Hammond tries out motor-home racing, while James May pits an Alfa Romeo 159 against a man who crosses the Humber estuary without using its famous bridge. The Stig puts Honda's Civic Type R through its paces and former England rugby union forward Lawrence Dallaglio is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.desc: Model Katie Price, comedian Greg Davies and Citizen Khan star Adil Ray tell Frank Skinner what really gets on their wick, in the hope he will banish their bugbears to the infamous room for ever. The celebrities' gripes include friends who give their dog specific instructions and talentless people on talent shows.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites another three celebrities to reveal their pet hates in the hope he will banish them to Room 101. Chef Heston Blumenthal hates it when people put milk in their tea first, Newsnight presenter Kirsty Wark is put off by men who wear too much aftershave and stand-up comedian James Acaster hates geese.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Jamali Maddix, Sindhu Vee, Fern Brady
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up Jamali Maddix comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by Fern Brady and Sindhu Vee.(n)

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: On the latest leg of his road trip, Zuu ventures into the unknown - travelling from his native West London, all the way to Hackney in East London to cook for comedian Jamali Maddix.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies invites all five contestants to have some global fun with a beach ball, while Lou Sanders has some wise words for the youth of today. Plus, Paul Sinha tries to invent a revolutionary new use for a basketball, and Sian Gibson attempts to rescue Little Alex Horne from the sharp beak of an aquatic bird.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Question master Stephen Fry continues the comedy quiz's exploration of subjects beginning with the letter L as he asks a range of fiendish questions on the topics of luck and loss. Joining regular panellist Alan Davies are Top Gear presenter Jeremy Clarkson and broadcasters Sandi Toksvig and Danny Baker, who are all hoping they don't fall into the QI elves' traps and set off the klaxon.(n)

programme.title: Mars the Magnificent
programme.desc: Documentary examining the search for wreckage from a 17th-century Swedish warship, as historians try to discover what destroyed the seemingly invincible vessel.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Nicholas Day investigates the murder committed by Ruth Ellis, the last woman to be hanged in Britain whose case divided the nation.(n)

programme.title: Hunting Down the Nazis
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about Simon Wiesenthal, a survivor of the Holocaust who dedicated his life to tracking down Nazi war criminals who had escaped justice.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two programmes from the Royal Hall, Harrogate, where Phillip Mould examines an ink drawing that he believes may have been made by Picasso. John Axford challenges Fiona to guess the odd one out from four tea bowls, and Jon Baddeley values a model ship that took more than 30 years to build.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Dufftown to Aviemore, learning how Victorian whisky trains were raided by robbers. He also travels along one of Scotland's most impressive viaducts and discovers that life is not always sweet on a shortbread production line.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the penultimate leg of his journey from Stirling to John O'Groats, Michael Portillo travels from Inverness to Plockton. Along the way he rides on one of the country's most picturesque railways, visits Scotland's smallest station and learns what a spa break in the Victorian era comprised.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Museum. Finds brought in to be valued include some jewellery from the art deco era, a painting by artistic group the Glasgow Girls, and an ugly family heirloom that turns out to have been made by a very important historical figure - and is worth far more than its owners suspected.(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The coral and shipwrecks found off the shores of the tropical islands, and how the different kinds of reef have given rise to a huge diversity of underwater life in the area. Underwater time-lapse photography also shows how carnivorous corals feed at night and how, over a few nights of the year, they disperse their sperm and eggs.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.sub-title: White Pass and Yukon Railroad - The Gold Rush Railway
programme.desc: The White Pass and Yukon Railroad over the US-Canada border, which began construction after the Klondike Gold Rush in the late 19th century and saw 35 workers lose their lives.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria.(n)

programme.title: Mission: Kill Hitler
programme.desc: A plot to kill the fuhrer that remained secret until the late 1990s. Operation Foxley planned to murder Hitler during his daily morning walk at the Berghof, his Bavarian residence. Two soldiers parachuted into the area were supposed to find a perfect location to shoot Hitler and stop the devastating war. Based on the British Secret Services' declassified documents, using archives and fictionalised reconstitutions, this film recounts the full story and the detailed plan that should have led to the murder of Adolf Hitler, and explains why it failed.(n)

programme.title: Hunting Down the Nazis
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary about Simon Wiesenthal, a survivor of the Holocaust who dedicated his life to tracking down Nazi war criminals who had escaped justice.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo starts off in Invergordon on the final leg of his journey to John O'Groats. Along the way, he learns how one man's vision helped bring train travel to the Highlands, discovers how farming has changed since Bradshaw's day and hears the remarkable tale of Scotland's Victorian gold rush.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows in the footsteps of engineer Isambard Kingdom Brunel as he travels from London to Newton Abbot in Devon. He begins by taking a trip from Paddington station to Warminster in Wiltshire, along the way learning about a Victorian asylum, giving a historic horse a facelift and making malt the 19th-century way.(n)

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a 19th-century schooner in Lake Erie off Ohio's coast that could shed light on not just the founding of Ohio, but the settlement of North America itself.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.sub-title: If You Go Down to the Woods
programme.desc: Marlene is distraught when Tyler and Earl the dog go missing. Boycie, Bryan and Jed form a search party, but hunting through the haunted woods at night proves a less than pleasant experience for the would-be rescuers. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene eagerly anticipates Tyler's A-level results, dreaming of a long and distinguished academic future for her son. He is not as enamoured with the idea as she is, until Elgin, Jed, Bryan and Mrs Cakeworthy inadvertently paint a picture of the life he could have had. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie reluctantly signs up for Farm Idol, a new reality TV show aimed at finding Britain's sexiest rural workers, while Mrs Cakeworthy becomes suspicious of Marlene when a well-dressed man visits her while her husband is away. Comedy, starring John Challis and Sue Holderness.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Hanbury Hall in Droitwich. Among the rarities brought in to be valued are a jacket designed by Peter Blake, which proves less valuable for being nibbled by a ferret, a nude painting and a sketch of Napoleon on his deathbed. programme.title: The Day They Dropped the Bomb

Featuring a minute-by-minute account of what happened on that tragic day, through archive footage and interviews with eyewitnesses who rarely speak about their experience of the bombing. Also interviewed is navigator Theodore Van Kirk, who was the last surviving member of the crew who dropped the bomb, before his death in 2014.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen welcome six new teams into the tent, and challenge them to prepare 24 perfectly uniform miniature classics - tarte chiboust vallée d'Auge and rice puddings. In the second round, they put their own spin on Florida's famous key lime pie, which must be decorated with towering edible sculptures. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden assessn the results and decide who will be going on to the next round.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The hospital documentary returns, focusing on the stories of two motorcyclists, which reveal how the bonds between loved ones can be strengthened through adversity. Prezmek may have life-changing spinal injuries following a high-speed motorcycle crash on the M4, forcing his wife Tatia to reflect on how their plans for the future may have to change. Teenager Leon undergoes a CT scan after crashing into a central reservation. While waiting for the results, his mother Janey thinks back to his school days.(n)

programme.title: Finding Your Feet (2017)
programme.desc: A wealthy woman's privileged existence is shattered when she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend. Seeking solace, she turns to her estranged sister, who lives in a London council estate, and finds a new lease of life in joining her community dance class. Comedy, starring Imelda Staunton, Celia Imrie, Timothy Spall and Joanna Lumley.(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play. He also discovers Chile's stunning architecture, and plans his hardest-ever build, an observatory.(n)

programme.title: Women Who Kill
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary tells the stories of Celeste Beard-Johnson and Dorice 'Dee Dee' Moore, so-called black widow killers who murdered for money. Beard-Johnson conspired with her lesbian lover to kill her millionaire husband, while Moore befriended and murdered a $30million lottery winner.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Parent?
programme.desc: Home-educators Becky and Ben believe that for their three daughters - aged 10, eight and five - learning should not just happen in a classroom but all the time, regardless of location. They go up against Richard and Emily, who follow a regimented parenting style based on a meticulous routine, which includes daily training on a running machine and music lessons for their sons, aged six and five. Completing this week's line up are Andi and Jon, who employ a 'structured chaos' approach to bringing up their two sets of twins, aged six and three, and a son aged four. Presented by Anita Rani.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The problem is Vanessa wants to settle in Lowestoft with family and friends nearby, while Gary wants to be in southern Spain where he can be close to Marbella's car scene. Help is at hand from Laura Hamilton, who delivers three homes in Lowestoft for their maximum £195,000 UK budget, while Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three amazing properties in the hills above Marbella with their £178,000 limit.(n)

However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table.(n)

programme.desc: A former army medic who worked for a private ambulance company tries to claw back thousands of pounds owed in wages. Lawrence Grix and Kev McNally also try to recoup some money for a wronged car buyer, only to find themselves locked in the garage that sold the vehicle.(n)

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The team faces a logistical nightmare when it rescues a mule in need of urgent medical attention, but then has to figure out how to transport it to HQ.(n)

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: A 14-year-old spaniel endures a traumatic experience trapped in a hole in the garden of a house.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and James Lewis begin their journey near Lacock in Wiltshire, travelling through Bath, Frome and Swindon in search of collectibles, before heading to an auction in Cirencester, Gloucestershire. Each antiques expert will be hoping to make the most profit when their bargains go under the hammer.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw's final trip in the 1960s Volvo sees them plot a course through Kent. Paul finds himself in Dover and hears about one plucky swimmer who believed bagpipes would help him across the English Channel, while Kate infiltrates a once top secret organisation for the afternoon to learn more about their history. The duo later head for an auction in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Natasha Raskin Sharp set off in their 1969 Morris Minor from the Firth of Clyde in search of bargains, making their way through the Scottish Borders to an auction in Carlisle.(n)

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi turns referee to settle a problem between Donna, the manager of Redditch hotel The Blue Inn, and the owner, who monitors her every move on CCTV cameras from her office more than 400 miles away in Paris. Despite having 15 staff, Donna works 100 hours a week and insists on getting too hands-on, so Alex asks her to devise a plan to get more heads on beds before they cross the Channel to see the boss.(n)

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Ronen has a huge but mysterious lump above his knee. Meanwhile, Amber is suffering from a debilitating skin disease that no one understands.(n)

programme.title: Ghost Loop
programme.desc: The work of a team of spirit hunters who visit different locations to rescue the living from a repetitive type of haunting known as a 'ghost loop'. They begin in Palatka, Florida, to help a mother who believes an unknown entity is crawling into her children's bedrooms.(n)

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: Zak and the crew investigate reports of ghoulish shrieks at a tuberculosis hospital-turned-hotel in Idaho.(n)

programme.title: Most Haunted

programme.desc: Birmingham pair Derek Hansel and Gina Awoko are looking for a home in Almeria, Spain, that they can eventually retire to and also generate some rental income from. However, it remains to be seen whether she can deliver the perfect property in the perfect location.(n)

programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part.(n)

programme.desc: Over 300 of the world's best white water rafters travelled to the Cassowary Coast for a chance to become the ' best of the best' in the International Rafting Federation's premier event of 2019.(n)

programme.title: The Fast Lady (1963)
programme.desc: A cycling enthusiast on a modest civil servant's salary gives up his beloved bike to a buy a luxury car to impress a local magistrate's daughter. However, he first has to pass his driving test to stand any chance of winning her heart - and in the process ends up in a wild car-chase after a gang of robbers. Comedy, with Julie Christie, Stanley Baker, James Robertson Justice, Leslie Phillips and Dick Emery.(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: London Districts

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Jimmy Carr and the Science of Laughter - A Horizon Special
programme.desc: The comedian takes over the science programme in a special episode for BBC's sitcom season, turning it into a chat show with eminent scientists as guests. He talks to them about the nature of laughter, with the studio audiences serving as guinea pigs in his attempt to get to the bottom of what laughter is, what it has to do with comedy, and why human beings find it a pleasurable sensation. The programme reveals that laughter predates the human species and may have played a vital role in our evolution.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden.(n)

programme.title: Europe at Sea ()

programme.title: Dying of the Light (2014)
programme.desc: A CIA agent is diagnosed with a degenerative condition that results in him being forced to retire from the world of intelligence. When he receives word that a terrorist who once tortured him has resurfaced, he goes rogue and sets out to assassinate the man while he is still able. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob.(n)

programme.title: Caravaggio (1986)
programme.desc: The Renaissance artist lies on his deathbed and remembers his tumultuous life. programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden.(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Ill Met By Moonlight (1957)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War thriller following the exploits of a squadron of British officers who head into Nazi-occupied Crete to kidnap a German general from under his comrades' noses, but struggle to transport their prisoner back to Cairo unnoticed. Starring Dirk Bogarde, Marius Goring and Cyril Cusack. Directed by frequent collaborators Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger.(n)

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home.(n)

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec gets on so well with Zoe's parents that she begins to feel left out.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: A London Community in Lockdown
programme.desc: A heartfelt look at life during lockdown in the 'Listraban' community in London, a record of a moment in time that will be in the history books for generations to come.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams looks at the role - and the unusual odd jobs - of country policemen in the South and South East during the Second World War, when rumours of chicken stealing and illegal pig roasts were fairly common diversions from the privations of rationing.(n)

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food.(n)

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food.(n)

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is sacked for dangling a customer by the ankles, while Gary, on the other hand, is offered a new post. However, he has difficulty breaking the news to George and Anthea. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: Essex Vendetta (2016)
programme.desc: An ordinary man is drawn into the dangerous world of organised crime as he strives to protect his family. His new life in the Essex underworld leads to him being sent on a murderous mission to Malta, where he discovers his partners in crime are not to be trusted. Crime drama, starring Michael McKell, Kris Johnson and Danniella Westbrook.(n)

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)
programme.desc: A gang of lads selling drugs in Darndale in Dublin's Northside sets out to gain more money and power and enter the big leagues of the narcotics trade. Things begin to unravel quickly as not everyone in the area is willing to let them achieve the notoriety they crave without a fight. Thriller, starring John Connors and Fionn Walton.(n)

programme.title: Hooligan Escape: The Russian Job (2018)
programme.desc: Five British football fans are arrested while attending the World Cup in Russia. However, their problems get even worse when a corrupt guard hands them over to an old enemy who has been plotting revenge for years. Thriller, starring Ali Bastian and Charlie Wernham.(n)

programme.title: Deep End (1970)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old boy gets a job working at a swimming pool, and discovers his work includes providing sexual services to the patrons in exchange for tips. He lusts obsessively after a woman who works alongside him, and begins plotting to break up her engagement. Comedy drama, starring John Moulder-Brown and Jane Asher.(n)

programme.title: Great SAS Missions
programme.desc: The raid by two SAS squadrons on Pebble Island in the Falklands during the 1982 conflict that led to the destruction of one of Argentina's most important air bases.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.sub-title: Forest Hill, Hither Green, Cubitt Town
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls catch up with their old friends Dee Dee and Dwayne at a surfing contest in Polynesia, but the event is attacked by a giant octopus.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls attend a roller-skating contest, and investigate when someone seems to be sabotaging the competition.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar thinks he has been blessed with the power to turn into animals - which results in a duck taking command of the ship when the Vikings all think it is their captain.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Piccadilly Incident (1946)
programme.desc: A Wren is pronounced missing in action, but returns three years later to find her husband has remarried. Melodrama, starring Anna Neagle and Michael Wilding.(n)

programme.title: Faking It

programme.title: Opening Up the Soane
programme.desc: Documentary about the extensive refurbishment of Sir John Soane's Museum to restore the building to its original glory.(n)

programme.title: Amy Winehouse: Her Legacy
programme.desc: The work of the charity set up in the singer's name to help young people struggling with addiction and mental health issues.(n)

programme.title: Bright Young Things (2003)
programme.desc: A would-be writer tries to raise enough money to marry his sweetheart, but a lost manuscript and several unlucky bets put his goal out of reach. Desperate to turn matters round, he launches himself into 1930s high society, hoping to curry favour with the rich and influential. Comedy based on Evelyn Waugh's Vile Bodies, directed by Stephen Fry and starring Emily Mortimer, Stephen Campbell Moore, James McAvoy and Michael Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
